metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default run-confirmation mechanism in AC1 (e.g. 'the bot stores the CI run ID before triggering and checks it matches before casting the vote') so the executor has a concrete design decision to implement, not invent.
findings.finding: AC1 leaves the run-confirmation mechanism as an unspecified design decision for the executor: 'confirming the specific combined-tip run passed' does not state whether this is done via run ID stored in a Gerrit message, a status API field, a database record, or another mechanism.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC1: 'The bot MUST record the CI run ID (or equivalent immutable handle) prior to submission, and verify that ID matches the run whose result it is acting on before casting Verified +1; no vote is cast if the IDs diverge.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reframe AC3 as an observable deliverable: specify the artifact the audit produces (e.g. 'a signed security review document at `docs/security/s8-verified-audit.md` containing: (1) atomic-submit soundness argument, (2) copied-vote path analysis with conclusion NONE, (3) ACL membership screenshot or dump, (4) gate-weakening analysis with conclusion NO CHANGE') so done-ness is unambiguous.
findings.finding: AC3 ('Security-invariant audit: soundness argument ... no copied-vote path ... bot-only ACL ... two-vote gate is not weakened') is effort-framed — it describes work to be done (conduct an audit, produce arguments) rather than a specific observable end-state deliverable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define and document the explicit interface/contract (event, webhook payload, API endpoint, or named CI artifact) by which the bot confirms the combined-tip run result, and reference it in the AC or a linked design note.
findings.finding: The plan specifies that the bot casts `Verified +1` ONLY after 'confirming the specific combined-tip run passed,' but the interface/contract between the CI system and the bot — the mechanism by which the bot learns a specific run's outcome — is unspecified. This is a new cross-component dependency introduced without an explicit event, API, or data-contract boundary.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — item 1: 'confirms the specific combined-tip run passed'; Why/What sections describing the bot's behavior

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly distinguish 'no copied-vote path' (covered by existing copyCondition) from 'no mis-targeted explicit bot-cast' (the new threat introduced by this story), and specify in AC1/AC3 the concrete mechanism (e.g., run-ID-to-patchset-SHA binding check before each cast) that prevents the bot from casting Verified+1 on a batch member whose SHA was not the tree tested by the combined-tip run.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'no copied-vote path' as a security property (AC1, AC3), treating this as covered by the existing GerriScary/copyCondition posture — but the actual threat in this story is an EXPLICIT bot-cast Verified+1 on non-tip batch members (changes that were NOT the tip the combined CI run built against). The existing copyCondition=NO_CODE_CHANGE guard prevents *carried* stale votes, not *directly cast* votes. No codebase artifact (project.config, ADR-0020, ADR-0023, gerrit-verify.yaml) implements or audits a guard against the bot mistakenly casting Verified+1 on a non-tip member using a run ID that tested a different SHA. These are architecturally distinct threat vectors.
findings.location: AC1 ('never a copied or stale vote — GerriScary posture') and AC3 ('no copied-vote path')
findings.suggested_fix: In AC1, replace 'never a copied or stale vote — GerriScary posture' with: 'The bot MUST verify that the patchset SHA of each batch member it votes on matches the SHA that was included in the combined-tip run (e.g., by recording [change-id, patchset-SHA] at batch-assembly time and asserting identity before casting). The GerriScary/copyCondition guard covers carried votes; this binding check covers directly-cast votes on non-tip members — a distinct and new threat vector.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and state in the soundness argument (AC3/ADR addendum) whether atomic submit relies on `submitWholeTopic` (requiring a shared topic) or on Gerrit relation-chain submit semantics, and confirm that the batching-assembly story guarantees the required topology (shared topic OR single relation chain) so the 'intermediate states never reach main' claim is grounded.
findings.finding: The plan's soundness argument 'atomic submit ⇒ intermediate states never reach main' (Why section) is an unverified assertion about the batching mechanism's Gerrit topology. The existing `submitWholeTopic = true` + FFO (ADR-0040) provides atomic submit only for a *linear topic*. The plan never asserts, and no codebase artifact confirms, that all batch members will share a single Gerrit topic — which is the required condition for `submitWholeTopic` to submit them atomically. Without this, the 'intermediate states never reach main' soundness argument has an unbacked premise.
findings.location: Why section: 'ancestor-atomic submit means the untested intermediate states never reach `main`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact Gerrit mechanism that delivers atomic submit (topic-based `submitWholeTopic` requiring a linear topology, or FFO relation-chain, or both) and confirm it is compatible with the batch topology that Phase 1 produces — referencing the constraint in ADR-0040 that parallel-sibling topic submit fails under FFO.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'submits atomically' and 'ancestor-atomic submit' but never specifies which Gerrit mechanism delivers that atomicity. The two deployed candidates — `submitWholeTopic=true` (requires all changes share a topic AND be linearizable) and FFO relation-chain submit — have different topological requirements, and ADR-0040 explicitly warns that 'a single topic containing parallel sibling changes to `main` cannot be linearized and its atomic submit fails.' If Phase 1's batch assembly produces a topology that doesn't match the mechanism assumed here (e.g., parallel-sibling topic vs. linear relation-chain), the 'atomic submit' step will fail silently or loudly at runtime rather than at design time.
findings.location: Why section ('ancestor-atomic submit means the untested intermediate states never reach `main`'); What section ('then submits atomically'); AC3 ('the two-vote gate is not weakened')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'What' section: 'Atomicity is delivered by Gerrit's `submitWholeTopic=true` (server config, already deployed) on a shared topic whose changes form a linear relation-chain — the topology Phase 1's batch-assembly story guarantees. Parallel-sibling topologies are excluded (per ADR-0040 Wikimedia limitation). The bot sets the topic on all batch members before submitting.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify — via Gerrit API documentation or a spike — that the bot can cast `Verified +1` on each batch member's post-assembly patchset from one CI run without triggering the 'copied vote' prohibition, and document this in the AC or ADR addendum (AC4).
findings.finding: The plan asserts the bot casts `Verified +1` on each batch member after the combined-tip CI passes, but the repo's proven `Verified`-casting path (ADR-0023, `gerrit-verify.yaml`) issues one SSH vote per patchset per CI run — one change, one run, one vote. Casting `Verified +1` on N non-tip batch members from a single combined-tip run is a novel multi-target vote mechanic that has no verified precedent in the codebase. The plan does not identify this as novel, cite any Gerrit API capability that supports a single run voting on multiple distinct change-numbers, or acknowledge the `copyCondition = NO_CODE_CHANGE` implication (any rebase that assembled the combined chain would have minted new patchsets, dropping prior `Verified` votes).
findings.location: What section: 'bot casts `Verified +1` on each batch member'; AC1: 'casts Verified +1 on batch members ONLY after confirming the specific combined-tip run passed'
findings.suggested_fix: The ADR addendum (AC4) should include: (a) the `gerrit review -m … --verified +1 <change-id>,<patchset>` invocation pattern for N members, (b) confirmation that each invocation targets the post-assembly patchset number (not a stale one), and (c) a statement that this is N independent fresh votes (not a copy), satisfying ADR-0020's `copyCondition = NO_CODE_CHANGE` requirement.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact run-confirmation protocol in AC1: name the artifact (run_id, SHA, Gerrit patchset revision) the bot uses to assert it is voting from the specific combined-tip run, and where that binding is recorded.
findings.finding: AC1 references 'confirming the specific combined-tip run passed' as a decision rule but never specifies the confirmation protocol: the existing `gerrit-verify.yaml` is dispatched per patchset-created and votes on the triggering patchset; no mechanism in the plan or codebase defines how the bot identifies, polls, or asserts a specific combined-tip run's conclusion before fan-out voting. This is a processing-protocol referenced without co-located specification.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: 'confirming the specific combined-tip run passed'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC1: 'Confirmation is by matching the GitHub Actions run_id (or equivalent immutable run handle) recorded at dispatch time against the run whose conclusion is being read; the bot stores this binding atomically with the dispatch and re-verifies it before casting any vote.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the fan-out vote protocol: enumerate the Gerrit API/SSH command used, how the bot maps each batch member to its current patchset number at vote time, the ordering constraint (if any), and the recovery action if any individual vote cast fails.
findings.finding: The plan says the bot 'casts Verified +1 on each batch member' but never specifies the fan-out voting protocol: which Gerrit API or SSH command, in what order, what the loop termination condition is, and how the bot knows which patchset number is current for each batch member at vote time. The existing `gerrit-verify.yaml` votes only on the patchset that triggered it; there is no fan-out vote path in the codebase.
findings.location: What section: 'the bot casts Verified +1 on each batch member'; Acceptance Criteria — AC2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In AC3, replace the 'GerriScary posture' reference with a concrete invariant specific to bot-programmatic vote fan-out: name the binding (e.g., SHA or run_id) that attests each non-tip batch member was included in the combined-tip run, and how that binding is verified before each fan-out vote is cast.
findings.finding: The plan invokes 'GerriScary posture' as a named security invariant in AC1 ('never a copied or stale vote — GerriScary posture') and AC3 ('no copied-vote path'), but the actual threat in this story is bot-programmatic vote fan-out (the bot intentionally casting Verified +1 on changes whose trees the combined-tip CI run did not directly test), which is orthogonal to the existing GerriScary/copyCondition mechanism. The plan never defines the concrete invariant that maps the GerriScary brand to this new threat model, creating an instruction-locality gap.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 ('GerriScary posture'), AC3 ('no copied-vote path')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit failure-path ACs: (a) if the combined-tip run fails, the bot casts Verified -1 on all batch members (fail-closed); (b) if a partial vote cast fails, the bot clears any already-cast votes and aborts the batch; (c) if atomic submit fails after all votes are cast, the bot clears votes and triggers re-assembly.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no fallback for an incomplete or failed sub-step in the Verified-casting sequence: if the combined-tip CI run passes but one or more fan-out `Verified +1` casts fail (SSH error, patchset-not-current, network timeout), no recovery path is defined. The plan also omits what happens if the combined-tip CI run itself fails — should the bot cast `Verified -1` on all batch members, on none, or on only the tip?
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1, AC3; What section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one concrete proving command per AC (e.g. for AC2: `ssh gerrit gerrit ls-members 'CI-Bot-Verified'` to confirm only the service account is in the group; for AC4: `test -f docs/adr/NNNN-verified-semantic-shift.md`), so done-ness is evidence-based rather than assertion-based.
findings.finding: None of the four ACs has a concrete proving command or artifact-check; every criterion is stated as a prose goal with no described verification step, violating E6's requirement that every completion-relevant claim have a concrete proving command.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all four ACs)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit ACs (and corresponding test criteria) for failure paths: bot must NOT cast Verified when the combined-tip run failed, when the run ID cannot be confirmed, or when the result is stale/ambiguous; specify the observable bot behavior in each case.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no failure-path or invalid-input test criteria for the Verified-casting bot: there is no AC or test plan covering what happens when the combined-tip CI run failed, when the run result is stale or ambiguous, or when the specific run ID cannot be confirmed — all critical guards for a gate-touching mechanism.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Add ACs such as: 'Bot emits no Verified vote and logs a structured error when run status is non-passing, stale, or unresolvable'; 'A test (integration or contract) exercises each of these paths and asserts no Verified vote is cast.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an observable proof mechanism for the 'two-vote gate is not weakened' invariant (e.g., an ACL smoke-test, an integration test that asserts non-bot Verified attempts are rejected, or a named audit step in the security review).
findings.finding: AC3 asserts 'the two-vote gate is not weakened' as a security invariant but specifies no observable, end-state proof mechanism — no test, CI check, audit-log assertion, or formal review sign-off is named that would confirm this invariant holds after the change.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'the two-vote gate is not weakened'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC or sub-step: 'A scripted or manual ACL check confirms that only the bot Service User account can cast Verified votes; this check is run as part of the security audit and its output is attached to the ADR addendum.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one live/end-to-end acceptance criterion that exercises the actual Gerrit Verified-casting path and ACL enforcement (e.g., a staging-environment smoke test or an integration test against a real/near-real Gerrit instance) so the suite cannot go green while the live path is broken.
findings.finding: The plan's security-invariant audit is framed solely as a 'soundness argument' (prose reasoning), with no live or end-to-end acceptance criterion that exercises the actual Gerrit vote-casting path or ACL enforcement — leaving the newly-defaulted live path validatable purely by offline/mock coverage, which would let the suite go green while the live guard is broken.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2, AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'An integration test against a Gerrit staging instance verifies (a) the bot Service User can cast Verified +1 after a passing combined-tip run, (b) a non-bot actor's attempt to cast Verified is rejected by ACL, and (c) no Verified vote appears when the run result is non-passing.' This makes the live path non-bypassable by offline mocks.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that exercises the atomic-submit path end-to-end (e.g. 'submit a two-member batch and verify `git log origin/main` contains no intermediate commit between the batch members') or explicitly defer it to a named child story with rationale.
findings.finding: AC3's safety claim 'atomic submit ⇒ intermediate states never reach main' is a live-path cutover claim with no end-to-end exercise criterion that exercises the actual submit path in production (or a staging environment that exercises the same code path), violating E6(f).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one concrete validation mechanism (e.g., an integration/end-to-end test that simulates a combined-tip run passing and asserts the bot casts Verified exactly once per batch member with the correct run ID, or a named operational metric to monitor post-rollout).
findings.finding: No concrete validation mechanism is specified for the gate-change working correctly end-to-end — there is no before/after workflow comparison, operational metric target, or integration test that would confirm the bot's Verified-casting and atomic submission behave as intended in a real or staging environment.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all four ACs

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Cite `submitWholeTopic = true` (ADR-0040) as the prior-art mechanism for atomic batch submit, address its FFO linearization constraint for multi-uploader batch members, and either confirm the batch-Verified mechanic builds on it or document why a separate atomic-submit path is needed.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'ancestor-atomic submit' (one combined-tip CI run satisfies `Verified` for all batch members) as sound, but does not engage with `submitWholeTopic = true` — the already-deployed Gerrit-native atomic-submit mechanism documented in ADR-0040 and ADR-0013 — as prior art. ADR-0040 explicitly flags 'a lightweight Gerrit commit-queue bot before Zuul' as the intended next step and warns that 'parallel sibling changes to `main` cannot be linearized' under FFO+topic. S8 implements exactly that next step but neither cites that prior-art pointer nor addresses the FFO linearization constraint for multi-uploader batches.
findings.location: Why section: 'ancestor-atomic submit means the untested intermediate states never reach `main`'; AC2: 'soundness argument (atomic submit ⇒ intermediate states never reach `main`)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define error-handling and retry behaviour for Gerrit API write operations: specify idempotency of re-casting `Verified +1`, recovery path when submit fails after votes are cast, and backoff policy for transient API failures.
findings.finding: The plan introduces Gerrit API write operations (cast `Verified +1` per batch member, then atomic submit) but specifies no error handling, retry/backoff, or recovery path for partial-batch failures — e.g., votes cast on some members before an API error, or votes cast but submit subsequently fails.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all ACs; 'What' section: 'casts Verified +1 on each batch member, then submits atomically'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'The bot's Verified-cast + submit sequence is idempotent: a re-run on the same combined-tip run re-casts the same vote (Gerrit no-ops) and retries submit; on submit failure after votes are cast, the bot logs the incident and does NOT abandon the votes, so the next scheduled run can retry submit without re-running CI.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan (e.g., a feature flag disabling batch-Verified casting so the gate reverts to per-change CI triggers, or an expand-contract sequence) to the plan so the semantic change to Verified is reversible.
findings.finding: The plan acknowledges the semantic breaking change to the Verified label (AC2, AC4) but provides no rollback/back-out plan or expand-contract sequence. Per T4, acknowledgement alone is insufficient — the plan must describe how to reverse the change if the batch-testing approach needs to be abandoned.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2, AC3; Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection under Scope: 'A feature flag (e.g., `batch_verified_casting_enabled`) gates the new mechanic; disabling it causes the bot to skip Verified casting, reverting to the pre-existing per-change CI trigger path. The flag is removed once the mechanic has been stable for N weeks.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and update all contributor-facing docs (CONTRIBUTING.md, developer guides, wiki pages) that describe `Verified +1` semantics to reflect the new 'tested as part of its landed batch' meaning, not just the ADR addendum.
findings.finding: The plan changes the public semantics of the `Verified +1` label (AC item 2 explicitly notes the meaning shifts to 'tested as part of its landed batch'), but only requires an ADR addendum — it does not address updating existing contributor-facing documentation (CONTRIBUTING.md, developer guides, wiki, or onboarding docs) that explain what `Verified` means. Those references become stale and will mislead contributors.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — item 2: 'documented that per-change Verified now means…' and item 4: 'An ADR addendum records the semantic shift'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit AC sub-item: 'Update CONTRIBUTING.md (and any linked developer guide / wiki page) to replace the per-change-isolation description of `Verified` with the batch-semantics description, cross-linking the ADR addendum.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC requiring the bot to emit a structured audit log entry (run-id, batch-member change IDs, vote cast timestamp, submit outcome) and a metric/alert for any vote-cast or submit failure.
findings.finding: The plan adds new failure points (Gerrit API calls for vote-casting and submit) but includes no requirement for observability — no metrics, logs, alerts, or audit trail beyond the ADR addendum — so operators cannot detect or diagnose bot misbehaviour or silent failures.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all four ACs); 'Security-invariant audit' AC

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/review-policy.md` to the plan's edit scope and update its Verified semantics section to reflect the batch-member meaning introduced by this story.
findings.finding: docs/review-policy.md is an unenumerated consumer of the Verified semantic change. The plan shifts Verified to mean 'tested as part of its landed batch,' but docs/review-policy.md (the authoritative per-the-doc-itself policy document) explicitly states Verified is cast 'against your exact patchset' — directly contradicted by the batch-member semantics. This document is not named anywhere in the plan's edit scope.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 ('documented that per-change Verified now means…')
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan's What/Scope section, explicitly name docs/review-policy.md as an edit target alongside the ADR addendum; the §Verified – CI sub-section needs a new paragraph explaining the batch-testing semantic.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `CONTRIBUTING.md` (specifically §2c and the Verified description block) to the plan's edit scope to reflect the batch-member Verified semantic.
findings.finding: CONTRIBUTING.md is an unenumerated consumer: it carries multiple per-patchset Verified semantic descriptions that the plan's batch-member shift directly contradicts. The plan names no update to CONTRIBUTING.md.
findings.location: Scope / What — edit targets

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate infra/runbooks/two-vote-gate-rollback.md and infra/runbooks/g2p-ci-credentials.md as consumers that need at minimum a note about the batch-member Verified semantic introduced by this story.
findings.finding: infra/runbooks/two-vote-gate-rollback.md and infra/runbooks/g2p-ci-credentials.md are unenumerated consumers. Both documents carry the current one-to-one Verified semantics (a run casts Verified on the single dispatching change) and the rollback instructions reference those semantics. Neither is named in the plan's edit scope.
findings.location: Scope — Phase 2 edit targets

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'two-vote gate' explicitly in AC3 (e.g. 'Code-Review +2 AND Verified +1 must both be present before submit') so the audit has an unambiguous invariant to verify.
findings.finding: The term 'two-vote gate' in AC3 is undefined jargon; the plan never specifies which two votes compose the gate, preventing an executor from verifying the invariant.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'the two-vote gate is not weakened' with 'the Code-Review +2 AND Verified +1 submit requirements remain unchanged — neither label is removed, defaulted, or bypassed by the bot ACL change.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a sentence to the What or Scope section explicitly listing the ADR addendum as a deliverable (e.g., 'Produce an ADR addendum documenting the semantic shift and the soundness argument') so AC4 maps to described work.
findings.finding: AC4 (ADR addendum recording the semantic shift + safety argument) is a concrete document deliverable with no corresponding description in the plan body (Why / What / Scope). The plan body never mentions producing an ADR or any documentation artifact, leaving AC4 as an orphan criterion whose presence or absence an implementer cannot derive from the described work.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4; plan body sections Why / What / Scope
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section, append: 'Produce an ADR addendum documenting: (a) the semantic shift in Verified label meaning, (b) the soundness argument (atomic submit ⇒ intermediate states never reach main), and (c) the no-copied-vote guarantee.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that vote-casting is idempotent for the same (run-id, change-id) pair and that concurrent bot executions are serialised or deduplicated (e.g., via a run-id idempotency key or Gerrit's native re-vote no-op guarantee).
findings.finding: The plan does not specify that the Verified +1 vote-casting operation is idempotent under at-least-once delivery (e.g., if the bot event-trigger fires twice for the same combined-tip run), leaving a concurrency/retry gap in the shared Verified label state lifecycle.
findings.location: AC1: 'The bot casts Verified +1 on batch members ONLY after confirming the specific combined-tip run passed'; 'What' section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific ADR file(s) to be addended in AC4 (e.g., docs/adr/0020-two-vote-ci-gate.md and/or docs/adr/0023-inbound-github-gerrit-ssh-vote.md), or clarify that a new ADR is to be created.
findings.finding: AC4 ('An ADR addendum records the semantic shift + the safety argument') names no specific ADR number or file path. The two most relevant ADRs (ADR-0020 at docs/adr/0020-two-vote-ci-gate.md, ADR-0023 at docs/adr/0023-inbound-github-gerrit-ssh-vote.md) are the obvious targets, but the plan leaves the edit target unresolved, making the deliverable ambiguous for implementers.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC4 to read: 'An addendum to ADR-0020 (docs/adr/0020-two-vote-ci-gate.md) records the semantic shift; the safety argument (soundness of ancestor-atomic submit) is recorded as a new §Consequences bullet or as ADR-0042.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the concrete artifact (script, workflow, or service component) that implements the multi-change Verified vote-cast, and confirm it exists or will be created, so AC1–AC3's security audit has a grounded subject.
findings.finding: The plan names no concrete file, module, script, or workflow for the new multi-change Verified vote-cast mechanism. The existing gerrit-verify.yaml casts Verified on exactly one change-number (the one whose patchset triggered the dispatch via gerrit-to-platform). Casting Verified on multiple batch members from a single combined-tip run requires a new code artifact, but the plan's What and Scope sections name no file path or implementation location for this new mechanism.
findings.location: What / Scope — 'the bot casts Verified +1 on each batch member'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale statement to the 'Why' or 'Scope' section explaining why a single combined-tip CI run (with the associated Verified semantic shift) was chosen over individual per-change CI runs with gated batch submit — e.g., CI cost reduction, throughput, or another concrete reason.
findings.finding: The plan provides no positive rationale for choosing the single-combined-tip-CI-run approach over the structurally different alternative of running individual CI per batch member and gating atomic submit on all passing. The 'Why' section gives the *soundness* argument for the chosen approach but not the *selection* rationale, leaving the implementer and auditor without a basis to evaluate whether the semantic shift in Verified's meaning (from 'tested in isolation' to 'tested as part of its batch') is the correct trade-off.
findings.location: Why section; Scope section ('The Verified-casting mechanic + its security audit')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name and verify the exact Gerrit API endpoint or SSH subcommand used to cast `Verified +1` (e.g., `POST /changes/{change-id}/revisions/{rev}/review` with payload `{"labels":{"Verified":1}}`), confirm it exists on the target Gerrit instance/version, confirm the bot Service User holds the `label-Verified` ACL permission, and classify the evidence as Verified (observed) before committing the full plan.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the bot will cast `Verified +1` on each batch member via Gerrit but names no specific Gerrit API endpoint, REST method, or SSH command used to post the label vote. Gerrit label-posting surfaces (e.g., `POST /changes/{id}/revisions/{rev}/review` with a `labels` payload) have non-obvious field names and scope requirements that differ by Gerrit version and by whether the label is configured as `COPY_ALL_SCORES_IF_NO_CHANGE`. Without naming the concrete endpoint/subcommand and verifying it exists in the target Gerrit instance's version, there is an unverified capability gap.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 & AC2 ('The bot casts Verified +1 on batch members ONLY after confirming the specific combined-tip run passed')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a subsection under 'What' that records: (1) the concrete endpoint/command, (2) the Gerrit instance version it was validated against, (3) the ACL permission entry verified, and (4) any label copy-policy setting that must be disabled to prevent stale-vote propagation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify and verify the Gerrit submit strategy (`Submit-Type`) configured for the target project, confirm it provides ancestor-atomic semantics (e.g., `MERGE_IF_NECESSARY` or explicit batch-submit endpoint), and document this dependency explicitly in both the plan and the ADR addendum.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'atomic submit' as the safety foundation of the entire security invariant ('ancestor-atomic submit means untested intermediate states never reach main') but names no specific Gerrit submit strategy or API surface (e.g., `Submit-Type: MERGE_IF_NECESSARY` vs `CHERRY_PICK` vs `REBASE_ALWAYS`, or the Gerrit Submit endpoint `POST /changes/{id}/submit`). Different submit strategies have different atomicity guarantees; cherry-pick, for example, does not guarantee ancestry atomicity in the same way as a merge strategy. This is an unverified capability gap.
findings.location: Why section ('ancestor-atomic submit means the untested intermediate states never reach main') and AC3 ('soundness argument (atomic submit ⇒ intermediate states never reach main)')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3: 'Submit strategy is confirmed as [X] in project.config; a change to submit strategy requires re-audit of the batch-submit invariant.' Reference the specific Gerrit Submit REST endpoint if used programmatically.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or ADR section) declaring the bot credential type, storage mechanism (e.g., CI secrets manager / Vault), rotation policy, and that the credential is scrubbed from all CI logs on the bot→Gerrit API path.
findings.finding: The plan does not declare how the bot Service User authenticates to the Gerrit API (credential type: HTTP password, OAuth token, SSH key, etc.), where those credentials are stored at rest, whether a secrets manager is used, or whether the credential appears in CI logs on the boundary-crossing path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Verified-casting is restricted to the bot Service User ACL' and Security-invariant audit bullet; 'What' section (bot casts Verified +1)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the security-invariant audit AC: 'Bot Service User credential is injected via [secrets manager / CI secret store], never committed to source, and masked in CI logs; rotation cadence is [N days]; confirm no raw token appears in job output or ADR.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the bot Service User's complete Gerrit ACL grant set in the ADR addendum (or a referenced access-control spec), affirming it holds ONLY the minimum permissions needed (Verified vote + Submit on the target branch); explicitly confirm it does NOT hold Code-Review voting rights or direct push rights.
findings.finding: The plan restricts Verified-casting to the bot Service User ACL but does not enumerate or bound the bot's full permission set on the Gerrit instance; a Service User capable of atomic submit may also hold Code-Review voting rights, push-to-refs rights, or other elevated grants that exceed the minimum required for this workflow.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Verified-casting is restricted to the bot Service User ACL; documented that per-change Verified now means…'
findings.suggested_fix: In the ADR addendum, add a table: 'Bot Service User ACL: Verified +1/-1 on refs/for/refs/heads/*, Submit on refs/heads/* (merge-if-necessary strategy only); Code-Review: none; Push: none; all other rights: none.' Reference the Gerrit project.config entry.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare in the plan (or ADR addendum) that bot→Gerrit API calls use HTTPS or SSH exclusively; if the deployment already enforces this at the infra level, a one-line note to that effect is sufficient.
findings.finding: The plan does not declare the transport protocol for bot→Gerrit API calls; if the internal Gerrit instance is reachable over plain HTTP (not HTTPS/SSH), the bot's privileged credential and vote actions cross an unencrypted network path.
findings.location: 'What' section — 'the bot (Service User) casts Verified +1 on each batch member, then submits atomically'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the security-invariant audit AC or ADR: 'Bot communicates with Gerrit exclusively over HTTPS (TLS 1.2+) or SSH; plaintext HTTP is disabled on the Gerrit listener.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
